focus: Micro-Phonetics Refinement — Flap T Mastery at Full Native Speed
focus_ar: تحسين الصوتيات الدقيقة — إتقان الفلاب T بالسرعة الطبيعية الكاملة
arabic_challenge: You learned the flap T/D mechanic in L1 Week 2. At L3, the goal shifts from 'can I produce this sound' to 'is this sound perfectly automatic, even in unfamiliar words, complex clusters, and truly spontaneous, emotionally-charged speech (debates, humor, storytelling).' Per blueprint Table 11, this week targets Silver/Gold-tier precision — the difference between 'clearly practiced' and 'invisible, unnoticed as an accent feature at all.'

target_sounds: flap T in unfamiliar, never-drilled words

isolation.instructions: Test your flap T on words you've never specifically drilled — true mastery means the rule applies automatically to ANY qualifying word, not just memorized examples.
isolation.instructions_ar: اختبر الفلاب T بتاعك على كلمات ماتدربتش عليها أبدًا — الإتقان الحقيقي يعني القاعدة بتنطبق تلقائيًا على أي كلمة مؤهلة، مش بس الأمثلة المحفوظة.
practice: totally, meditate, capital, satellite, latitude, vital (all flap T, likely unfamiliar)

sentence_practice: It's totally vital to meditate about the capital's latitude.
The satellite orbits at a vital, totally specific latitude.
Meditate on the capital's totally vital importance.
daily_drills.record_this: It's totally vital to meditate about the capital's latitude and the satellite orbiting it.
daily_drills.self_check: Did the flap apply automatically to these unfamiliar words, without you consciously planning it?

isolation.instructions: Test whether the flap survives when you're emotionally engaged — angry, excited, or passionately arguing — since genuine emotion often disrupts careful articulation habits.
isolation.instructions_ar: اختبر لو الفلاب بيفضل موجود وانت متأثر عاطفيًا — غاضب، متحمس، أو بتجادل بحماس — لأن الانفعال الحقيقي غالبًا بيعطل عادات النطق الحذرة.
practice: Deliver an emotionally passionate 30-second rant about a minor annoyance, watching for flap T throughout.

pair: flap T surviving under real emotion
flap T lost under emotional pressure

daily_drills.record_this: (Free speech: a passionate 30-second rant about a minor daily annoyance, e.g., slow wifi or bad traffic.)
daily_drills.self_check: Did the flap T survive even when your focus was on the emotion/content, not the pronunciation?

target_sounds: flap T in complex consonant environments

isolation.instructions: Test flap T in words with additional nearby consonant complexity, which can sometimes disrupt the smooth tongue-tap motion.
isolation.instructions_ar: اختبر الفلاب T في كلمات فيها تعقيد ساكن إضافي قريب، ده ممكن يعطل حركة طرقة اللسان السلسة أحيانًا.
practice: instantly, constantly, distinctly, quantity (T near other consonant clusters)

sentence_practice: I instantly and distinctly remember the quantity constantly changing.
Constantly and instantly, the quantity was distinctly different.
The distinctly large quantity changed constantly, almost instantly.
daily_drills.record_this: I instantly and distinctly remember the quantity constantly changing — it was a distinctly large amount.
daily_drills.self_check: Did the flap hold up even with the extra consonant complexity surrounding it?

target_sounds: flap T integrated with humor/sarcasm delivery (thematic tie-in)

isolation.instructions: Apply flap T naturally while delivering a sarcastic or humorous remark (this week's vocabulary theme), since comedic timing often speeds up delivery in ways that test automaticity.
isolation.instructions_ar: طبّق الفلاب T بشكل طبيعي وانت بتقدم تعليق ساخر أو فكاهي (موضوع مفردات هذا الأسبوع)، لأن التوقيت الكوميدي غالبًا بيسرّع الأداء بطرق تختبر التلقائية.
practice: Oh, TOTALLY, that's a GREAT idea. (sarcastic, flap on 'totally')

sentence_practice: Oh, TOTALLY, that's a GREAT idea. (sarcastic)
daily_drills.record_this: Oh, TOTALLY, that's a GREAT idea — definitely, I TOTALLY believe that, absolutely.
daily_drills.self_check: Did the flap survive the exaggerated, playful delivery required for effective sarcasm?

target_sounds: flap T in fully spontaneous, unscripted 60-second speech

isolation.instructions: The ultimate test: 60 seconds of completely spontaneous speech on any topic, with zero preparation, checking whether flap T remains fully automatic.
isolation.instructions_ar: الاختبار النهائي: ٦٠ ثانية كلام عفوي كامل عن أي موضوع، بدون تحضير، لفحص لو الفلاب T فضل تلقائي بالكامل.
practice: Speak spontaneously about literally anything for 60 seconds.

pair: fully automatic flap in spontaneous speech

daily_drills.record_this: (Free speech: 60 seconds, completely unplanned, any topic.)
daily_drills.self_check: Count how many flap-T opportunities appeared in your speech, and what percentage you executed correctly WITHOUT thinking about it.

daily_drills.target_sounds: Review: unfamiliar words, emotional pressure, consonant complexity, humor integration, full spontaneity
challenge_sentences: It's totally vital to instantly and distinctly remember the satellite's latitude — oh, TOTALLY, that's a great idea, definitely.
The capital's quantity constantly changed, and it was distinctly, totally unexpected.
Meditate on the vital importance, instantly and constantly, of a totally different latitude.
daily_drills.record_this: It's totally vital to instantly and distinctly remember the satellite's latitude — oh, TOTALLY, that's a great idea, definitely!
daily_drills.self_check: This is a demanding review passage combining all 5 days' challenges. Score your flap-T consistency honestly.

test_yourself.instructions: Record 60-90 seconds of spontaneous speech on any topic. Then, separately, have a native-speed conversation (real or imagined roleplay) reacting with genuine emotion/humor at least twice.
test_yourself.passage: (Free speech: 60-90 seconds on any topic, PLUS a brief emotional/humorous reaction moment within it.)

sounds_to_check.flap T automaticity: check throughout for consistency without conscious effort

scoring_guide.90_100: (Gold/Platinum tier) Flap T is completely invisible as an 'effort' — indistinguishable from unconscious native production, even under emotion and in unfamiliar words.
scoring_guide.70_89: (Silver tier) Flap T holds up in almost all contexts; very rare lapses under high emotional pressure.
scoring_guide.50_69: Flap T reliable in calm, familiar speech but noticeably drops under emotional pressure or with new vocabulary.
scoring_guide.below_50: Flap T still requires conscious effort — return to L1 Week 2 for foundational review.

for_arabic_speakers: This week reframes a skill you already have (flap T from L1) through the lens of Blueprint Table 11's Mastery Tiers — the goal now is invisibility, not just correctness.

Testing a skill under emotional pressure and with unfamiliar vocabulary is the real test of whether it has become truly automatic (procedural memory) versus still being consciously monitored (declarative knowledge).
This same refinement approach (Days 1-5 structure) will repeat for other L1/L2 skills across L3's remaining weeks — dark L, schwa, and vowel reduction each get similar deep-mastery treatment.
supplementary_notes.recording_requirement: Record Day 5 and Day 7 — save these as your first L3 mastery-tier benchmark recordings.
